summary: Iteration 10 — Bug fix VERIFIED: legacy user (smoketest@vaulted.app, created pre-BIP39) multichain backfill works. Backend: 19/19 pytest pass (test_iteration10.py). The bug repro is solid — first call to /api/wallet/btc/info for the legacy user returns 200 with testnet3 address 'mnnh28wgbBz7JXqTtJv38HvnwUgANEw3ko' (P2PKH testnet3 'm/n'-prefix). /api/wallet/sol/info returns 200 with devnet base58 'HqdMsQ7kwyaXg6YgjNWCtYzNpmhPDncKZsRgm9QFhZTQ'. Critical invariant: ETH wallet_address recorded BEFORE the backfill (0x1b92496c0847ceede67d53161b1dd17a03b17cbf) matches BYTE-FOR-BYTE after — backfill only adds eth_mnemonic + btc_address + sol_address to the user doc, never touches the existing ETH key. Idempotent: 2nd call to /btc/info and /sol/info returns the same addresses (since user.btc_address + user.sol_address are now cached on the user doc, _ensure_multichain_addresses short-circuits at line 519). /wallet/assets surfaces all three addresses consistently. Fresh-user regression (TestFreshUserStillWorks): registration → /btc/info, /sol/info, /usdc/info, /assets all 200 with correct address shapes + Sepolia USDC contract 0x1c7d4b196cb0c7b01d743fbc6116a902379c7238. Iter9 regression: /api/health GET+HEAD 200, contacts alphabetical [Daniel Park, Maya Chen, Vault Support], /usdc/send invalid-address & short-hex 400, /usdc/send valid-address zero-balance 400 'Insufficient', /chat/send_crypto 1-on-1 no 5xx, /transactions/export streams text/csv with Date,Asset header. Frontend (390x844 mobile viewport): logged in as smoketest via the actual /login screen (testIDs login-email-input/login-password-input/login-submit-button), routed to /wallet, then deeplinked to /receive?asset=BTC. BTC chip shows 'Bitcoin · Testnet P2PKH' badge + QR + the legacy backfill testnet3 address + coinfaucet.eu faucet card. SOL chip 'Solana · Devnet ED25519' + QR + faucet.solana.com. ETH and USDC chips share the SAME 0x1b92… Sepolia address (preserved). No red-screen / 'Uncaught Error' overlay anywhere. Copy button dispatches without crash. The frontend belt-and-suspenders error UI (assetErr branch at receive.tsx:121-125) never had to trigger for smoketest because the backfill path now succeeds — exactly as designed.

minor.issue: Known: 28-byte JWT_SECRET (InsecureKeyLengthWarning), bcrypt __about__ AttributeError — both flagged in agent_to_agent_context_note as known. Not failing tests.
Carry-over: 1983 lines, past the 700-line guideline. Splitting into routers/ (auth, wallet, multichain, chat, stripe) still recommended.

action_items: (Carry-over, no action needed now) Set 32+ byte JWT_SECRET; pin bcrypt==4.0.1; consider splitting server.py into routers/.
critical_code_review_comments: _ensure_multichain_addresses (server.py:514-540) is correct: (a) short-circuits on user.btc_address & user.sol_address — guarantees idempotency without re-deriving; (b) prefers existing eth_mnemonic/mnemonic, falls back to Account.create_with_mnemonic() for legacy users — only sets eth_mnemonic in the update dict when it was missing, so users that already had a mnemonic keep theirs; (c) the ETH wallet_address field is never touched in this function — verified by the eth_addr_before == eth_addr_after assertion (TestLegacyBackfill::test_eth_address_unchanged_after_backfill). The backfill is therefore safe for legacy users.

Receive.tsx (line 60-67) catches the API error and translates 'mnemonic missing' into a friendly cream-colored explainer rendered at line 121-125 (qrErrText, color: colors.brandDeep). The qrErrText style has fontSize:12, lineHeight:16, textAlign:'center', fontWeight:'500' — good visual hierarchy. errByAsset is keyed per-Asset so error on BTC doesn't poison ETH state. No retry button, but switching chips re-runs the effect.

Minor: errByAsset is checked in the effect's guard (line 55) so once an asset has errored we never retry. Acceptable since the backfill now makes the primary path succeed; legacy users no longer hit this branch.

BIP-39 backfill writes eth_mnemonic to the user doc but the existing ETH private key/address is unchanged — there's now a 'split-derivation' state where ETH was generated pre-BIP39 (random 32-byte key) and BTC/SOL are derived from a fresh mnemonic. The eth_mnemonic field for these legacy users does NOT derive the existing ETH address. If a user ever exports the mnemonic via /wallet/eth/mnemonic expecting to restore their ETH funds elsewhere, this would silently mislead them. Worth a future cleanup or doc-string warning on _ensure_multichain_addresses about the semantic — but NOT blocking this iteration since send/receive on BTC/SOL is the only thing using the mnemonic-derived path and the smoketest fix lands the primary goal.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration10.py (new — 19 tests: TestLegacyBackfill x7, TestFreshUserStillWorks x4, TestIter9Regression x8)

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 4 chips on the receive screen render correct addresses for the legacy smoketest user; ETH preserved; no error overlay

seed_data_creation: One fresh user TEST_iter10_<uuid>@vaulted.app via /api/auth/register for the FreshUserStillWorks suite; legacy user smoketest@vaulted.app was used as-is for the backfill repro+verification. Backfill mutates ONLY {eth_mnemonic if missing, btc_address, sol_address} on the user doc — ETH wallet_address preserved.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter10 fixed the legacy-user crash by adding a mnemonic backfill to _ensure_multichain_addresses (server.py:514). Backfill is idempotent (short-circuits when btc_address+sol_address exist). For UI testing: log in via /login → routes to /wallet → deeplink to /receive?asset={ETH|USDC|BTC|SOL}. testIDs: receive-asset-{SYMBOL}, receive-address, receive-copy, receive-share, receive-faucet. The frontend safety-net error UI lives at receive.tsx:121-125 (qrErrText) and renders when api() throws — to test it, you'd need a server-side error path that returns 4xx, which post-fix is no longer the case for legacy mnemonic-missing scenario. NOTE on semantic risk (carried in critical_code_review_comments[3]): legacy users now have an eth_mnemonic that does NOT derive their actual ETH address — main agent may want to add a 'mnemonic_origin' field (pre_bip39 vs onboarded) or surface a warning on /wallet/eth/mnemonic for these users in a future iteration.
rca_of_issue: BUG (now FIXED): Legacy users (e.g., smoketest@vaulted.app) were created before BIP-39 mnemonic storage was introduced, so user.eth_mnemonic was missing. /wallet/btc/info and /wallet/sol/info called _ensure_multichain_addresses which returned {'btc': None, 'sol': None}, then the endpoint raised HTTPException 400 'No BTC/SOL address (mnemonic missing)'. The receive screen UI surfaced this as an uncaught Error. FIX (server.py:521-530): when mnemonic is None, _ensure_multichain_addresses now calls Account.create_with_mnemonic() to mint a fresh BIP-39 phrase, writes it to user.eth_mnemonic, derives BTC+SOL via derive_addresses(mnemonic), and persists btc_address/sol_address on the user doc. Critically, user.wallet_address (the pre-existing ETH key generated from a raw private key) is NEVER touched by this function — verified by reading eth_info BEFORE and AFTER calling /btc/info and asserting byte-for-byte equality (test_eth_address_unchanged_after_backfill). Idempotency: subsequent calls hit the short-circuit at line 519 since btc_address + sol_address are now cached. Belt-and-suspenders (receive.tsx:60-67) translates a 'mnemonic missing' error string into a friendly explainer instead of crashing — kept in place though the primary fix means it won't trigger for smoketest anymore.
